title: rubus corchorifolius var. palmatoides
intro: Rubus corchorifolius var. palmatoides, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of bramble native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The plant is also valued for its potential health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, improving digestion, and reducing oxidative stress. In traditional herbal medicine, Rubus corchorifolius var. palmatoides is often used to prepare infusions, teas, and tonics, and its leaves and stems can be used to make dried powder or extracts for various medicinal applications.
